description:
Analyze the structural basis of CRISPR-Cas9 target recognition using the SpCas9-sgRNA-DNA complex (PDB: 4UN3). Fetch or load PDB 4UN3 into PyMOL. Identify the four distinct polymer chains (Cas9 protein, sgRNA, Target DNA, Non-target DNA) and color them distinctly. Locate the PAM sequence (5'-NGG-3' motif) on the non-target DNA strand, and highlight the specific Arginine residues in Cas9 that read the PAM via major groove interactions (Arg1333 and Arg1335). Display the PAM nucleotides and these two interacting Arginines as sticks. Render a high-resolution PNG figure of this interaction and save it to /home/ga/PyMOL_Data/images/cas9_pam_recognition.png. Additionally, write a structural report to /home/ga/PyMOL_Data/cas9_pam_report.txt containing:
1. The mapping of each macromolecule to its Chain ID (e.g., Cas9=..., sgRNA=..., Target DNA=..., Non-target DNA=...)
2. The specific residue numbers of the two PAM-interacting Arginines
3. The exact 3-nucleotide PAM sequence present in this specific structure (the specific 5'-NGG-3' sequence located on the non-target strand).
